title: Death and Rebirth End Of Evangelion Commentary Track
display_title: Death and Rebirth End Of Evangelion Commentary Track
wikitext:
[]: Death and Rebirth/End Of Evangelion Commentary Track             Quote Post by KhakiBlueThunderSocks » Fri Sep 29, 2006 11:48 am It seems as though I've grown addicted to watching D&R and EoE with the commentary track playing in the background. For some reason, I understand more with the commentary track on then when I do with it off! Am I the only one who's like this, or am I just a crazy black Eva fan?

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Fri Sep 29, 2006 12:29 pm For the love of all that is good in this world turn those commentaries off. Everything you think is making sense is utter nonsense. The Manga fools are as dumb as the ADV fools when it come to explaining the series.

[]:             Quote Post by KhakiBlueThunderSocks » Fri Sep 29, 2006 12:37 pm Hunter21 wrote: For the love of all that is good in this world turn those commentaries off. Everything you think is making sense is utter nonsense. The Manga fools are as dumb as the ADV fools when it come to explaining the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Fri Sep 29, 2006 1:03 pm KhakiBlueThunderSocks wrote: "I'm never going to work in Hollywood, am I?" -Amanda "No, you're not." -Jason

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Sep 29, 2006 1:14 pm Commentary = Blech Don't watch it. The only one that actually has some useful information is the Platinum commentary on episode 1 by Matt Greenfield.

[]:             Quote Post by KhakiBlueThunderSocks » Fri Sep 29, 2006 3:30 pm I can't help it! I want to try and watch the movie without commentary (Especially on D&R because the disk is two sided and the side with the extras has no subtitles to follow along with), but whenever I put the disk in my DVD player, I always wind up flipping it and hitting "Audio Commentary"! I mean, it's one of the main selling points for me! That and it's Eva, of course.

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Fri Sep 29, 2006 4:55 pm the commentaries make me sad :cry: they all only think they know stuff because they dubbed it.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Fri Sep 29, 2006 6:39 pm The commentaries make my ears bleeding.. :x Seriously!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Sep 29, 2006 11:03 pm KhakiBlueThunderSocks wrote: but they did have some feedback about the movie that helped me form some of my own theories!

[]:             Quote Post by Mundane » Fri Sep 29, 2006 11:08 pm Reichu, I believe Eva Monkey or yourself had a picture of part of the commentary that had a mention at the horn on Eva 01's head as a phallic reference and one of them questioning what "phallic" meant. I had a good chuckle from that. I also believe it's appropriate for the current occasion.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Sep 29, 2006 11:47 pm That was for Greenfield and McCoy's "Mythology of Evangelion" feature, not AWL's Commentary of Evil. http://www.evacommentary.org/images_capdoc/dr-nick_greenfingers.jpg

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Fri Sep 29, 2006 11:58 pm Reichu wrote: That was for Greenfield and McCoy's "Mythology of Evangelion" feature, not AWL's Commentary of Evil. http://www.evacommentary.org/images_capdoc/dr-nick_greenfingers.jpg

[]:             Quote Post by KhakiBlueThunderSocks » Sat Sep 30, 2006 12:15 am Seems as though I've joined the right forum...I can have a healthy Evangelion discussion for once! Personally, I think AWL did the best she could do with the Evangelion movies...granded she did make a few missteps (I.E -- Adding the splooshing sound effects to EoE) but I think that she did a pretty good job.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Sat Sep 30, 2006 12:30 am KhakiBlueThunderSocks wrote: Seems as though I've joined the right forum... Personally, I think AWL did the best she could do with the Evangelion movies ...

[]:             Quote Post by Toilet Duck » Sat Sep 30, 2006 12:39 am monkey already posted a clip on the main site about the "slap/gunshot" thing. really, you'd think that if they had only PAID ATTENTION TO Asuka'S BEHAVIOR IN THAT SCENE, there wouldn't be that problem.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Sep 30, 2006 12:43 am KhakiBlueThunderSocks wrote: Personally, I think AWL did the best she could do with the Evangelion movies...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Sep 30, 2006 1:10 am HIT 'EM AGAIN!!! KhakiBlueThunderSocks wrote: Seems as though I've joined the right forum...I can have a healthy Evangelion discussion for once!

[]:             Quote Post by Mundane » Sat Sep 30, 2006 1:22 am KaworuisagirlthesplashnoisewascoolAWListhegreatestthingtohappensinceIcedEarth. There, I said it.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Sat Sep 30, 2006 1:59 am Reichu wrote: ... (Unless you say damn fool crazy things like Kaworu is a girl or whatever. )

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Sep 30, 2006 3:06 am If I were a guy, I would hit that Kaworu chick harder than the suckage of the Commentary of Evil. And I don't mean the Kaworu depicted above. (Mmm. Delicious flat chests.) I'm glad I'm not a guy.
summary:
[]: Death and Rebirth/End Of Evangelion Commentary Track             Quote Post by KhakiBlueThunderSocks » Fri Sep 29, 2006 11:48 am It seems as though I've grown addicted to watching D&R and EoE with the commentary track playing in the background. For some reason, I understand more with the commentary track on then when I do with it off! Am I the only one who's like this, or am I just a crazy black Eva fan?
